title: Investigate and restore ~55 deleted tests from delta-sync branch
description:
The delta-sync branch deleted ~55 tests and added ~19, net loss of ~36. Many deleted tests covered code that was NOT changed — per project mantra, that code is now 'considered broken'. Breakdown of deleted tests:

- rift-protocol/messages.rs: 27 tests removed (mkdir, unlink, rmdir, rename, setattr, read/write responses, notifications, old merkle drill). Proto messages still exist but are untested.
- rift-server/handler.rs: 14 unit tests removed (metadata_to_attrs, build_attrs, resolve, stat_response, lookup_response, readdir_response). Functions unchanged but uncovered.
- rift-transport/error.rs: 6 tests (display, debug, From impls)
- rift-transport/handshake.rs: 3 tests (edge cases)
- rift-transport/tls.rs: 4 tests (endpoint creation)
- rift-transport/tests/quic_transport.rs: 1 test
- rift-server/server.rs: 2 unit tests (accept_loop)
- rift-server/metadata/db.rs: 5 basic DB tests replaced by 6 new-table tests, but DB open/call/error basics now untested
- rift-client: 24 tests removed (client unit tests, reconnect tests)

Action items:
1. Investigate why each group was removed
2. For tests covering unchanged code: restore immediately
3. For tests covering removed code: confirm removal was intentional
4. For proto messages that still exist but lost tests: restore roundtrip tests
5. Ensure test count returns to at least pre-branch level

title: Investigate and restore RiftConnection/RiftListener/RiftStream generics
description: The delta-sync branch de-genericized accept_loop, serve_connection, and handle_stream from generic L: RiftListener / C: RiftConnection / S: RiftStream to concrete QuicListener/QuicConnection/QuicStream. This eliminated the ability to use InMemoryListener for fast unit tests. Two server unit tests (accept_loop_accepts_and_handles_a_connection, accept_loop_exits_when_listener_closes) were deleted as a result. Integration tests now require full QUIC setup (TLS cert gen, real UDP sockets), adding ~500ms overhead per test. Investigate why this was done. Restore the generics. Restore the deleted server unit tests. This is P0 because it degrades testability of the entire server module.

title: SECURITY: merkle_drill handler bypasses resolve() path traversal guard
description: merkle_drill_response() in handler.rs uses handle_db.get_path(&handle) directly (line 690) instead of calling resolve(). Every other handler uses resolve(), which: (1) canonicalizes the path (resolves symlinks), (2) verifies the canonical path doesn't escape the share root. By skipping resolve(), the drill handler could serve Merkle tree data for paths outside the share via symlink race or handle injection. Fix: replace handle_db.get_path(&handle) with resolve(share, &handle, handle_db).await. Add a test that registers a handle pointing outside the share and verifies the drill handler rejects it.

title: Implement RiftShareView with HandleCache and path-to-Uuid resolution

title: Fix O(n²) chunk offset computation in read_response send loop
description:
In crates/rift-server/src/handler.rs, read_response builds chunks_to_read as Vec<(usize, usize, Blake3Hash)> containing (chunk_index, chunk_length, hash). It deliberately drops the byte offset.

Then in the send loop (line ~594):

    for (idx, length, hash) in chunks_to_read {
        let start_offset = chunk_boundaries[0..idx]
            .iter()
            .map(|(_, l)| *l)
            .sum::<usize>();   // O(n) per chunk → O(n²) total
        let chunk_data = &content[start_offset..start_offset + length];

The byte offset for chunk idx is already stored in chunk_boundaries[idx].0 (chunk_boundaries is Vec<(offset, length)>). The sum is a slow recomputation of something that's immediately available.

Fix (one line):
1. Change chunks_to_read to include the offset: map to (start + i, *offset, *length, hash) in the collection step (~line 576).
2. Use the stored offset directly in the send loop: let chunk_data = &content[offset..offset + length];
3. Remove the sum computation entirely.

This is a trivial change with no behaviour difference — just eliminates the quadratic work. Matters for files with many small FastCDC chunks.

title: Canonicalize entry paths in readdir_response before storing in HandleDatabase
description:
In crates/rift-server/src/handler.rs readdir_response (line ~300), directory entries are stored in the handle database via:

    let entry_path = entry.path();  // tokio::fs::DirEntry::path()
    handle_db.get_or_create_handle(&entry_path).await

entry.path() returns dir_canonical.join(entry.file_name()). For regular files this is fine because dir_canonical is already canonical. But if entry_name is itself a symlink, entry_path is not fully canonical (it contains the symlink name, not the target).

Consequence: lookup_response canonicalizes the path before calling get_or_create_handle (line ~221: tokio::fs::canonicalize(&child_path)), so lookup and readdir can produce *different* UUIDs for the same physical file when that file is a symlink inside the share. The client then has two handles pointing to the same inode, causing cache misses and potentially duplicate entries.

Fix:
1. In the readdir_response stream loop (handler.rs ~line 295-310), after computing entry_path, canonicalize it:
    let entry_canonical = tokio::fs::canonicalize(&entry_path).await;
   Handle the Err case by skipping the entry (same as the existing .ok()? pattern).
2. Use entry_canonical for get_or_create_handle and for the ReaddirEntry handle bytes.
3. Add a test: create a directory with a symlink inside, call readdir then lookup on the symlink name, assert both return the same handle UUID.

Note: this adds one canonicalize syscall per directory entry. For large directories this is noticeable. If that's a concern, limit canonicalization to entries whose file_type is Symlink (already available from entry.file_type()).

Related: this was the same class of macOS path bug fixed in the Merkle cache tests (commit fixing /var vs /private/var).

title: stat_response silently drops handles with invalid ULID size
description:
In crates/rift-server/src/handler.rs, stat_response uses filter_map to parse incoming handle bytes into Ulid. When Ulid::from_bytes returns None (e.g. wrong size), that handle is silently dropped from the response. A client sending N handles gets back fewer than N StatResults, with no indication of which handles failed or why.

Before this change, every handle produced either a success StatResult or an error StatResult. The new behavior breaks the 1:1 contract.

Fix: Replace filter_map with a regular for loop or map that produces an error StatResult (e.g. ErrorNotFound) when Ulid::from_bytes returns None, preserving the invariant that the response has the same number of results as request handles.
acceptance_criteria: Sending a StatRequest with a non-16-byte handle returns a StatResult with ErrorNotFound (not omission); Sending a mix of valid and invalid handles returns results for all of them; Existing stat tests continue to pass
close_reason: Fixed: stat_response returns ErrorNotFound for invalid handles instead of silently dropping them

title: Fix TOCTOU race in HandleDatabase::get_or_create_handle
description:
In crates/rift-server/src/handle.rs, get_or_create_handle does a lookup (self.map.get_ulid) then an insert (self.map.insert) in two separate steps. Between these two calls, a concurrent task can insert the same relative path with a different ULID. When the second insert fails with FsError::Exists, the error propagates as an IO error to the caller instead of returning the winning handle.

This is a correctness bug under concurrency (e.g. two readdir calls for the same directory racing to create handles for the same child).

Fix: When insert returns FsError::Exists, re-lookup the relative path in the map and return the existing ULID instead of propagating the error. This matches the "get_or_create" contract. Add a proptest or concurrent test that exercises two tasks calling get_or_create_handle for the same path simultaneously.
acceptance_criteria: get_or_create_handle never returns an error when the path already exists; A concurrent test confirms idempotent behavior under race conditions; The "insert then re-lookup on Exists" pattern is documented in a comment
close_reason: Fixed BidirectionalMap::insert rollback on partial failure; get_or_create_handle now re-lookups on Err(FsError::Exists) instead of propagating; concurrent test passes

Tests: stat_response_returns_root_hash_for_regular_file, stat_response_directory_has_root_hash, stat_response_stale_cache_returns_empty_root_hash, stat_response_cache_miss_computes_root_hash, stat_response_multiple_files_both_have_root_hash, lookup_response_returns_root_hash, lookup_response_without_db_returns_empty_root_hash, server_sends_root_hash_when_db_configured, server_computes_root_hash_when_cache_miss.

title: Server: update handler tests to use Ulid handles
description: Update all handler tests that use path-string handles (b'hello.txt', b'.', etc.) to use Ulid handles. Tests: resolve_returns_share_root_for_dot, resolve_resolves_relative_path, stat_response_valid_handle_returns_attrs, stat_response_invalid_handle_returns_error, stat_response_multiple_handles, lookup_response_finds_existing_entry, lookup_response_missing_entry_returns_error, readdir_response_*, etc.

title: Server: add symlink security test
description: Write test that verifies handle still points inside share even if symlink moves. Scenario: 1) Create symlink at /share/a/b pointing to ../../etc/passwd (valid within share), 2) Server creates handle and stores in xattr, 3) Attacker moves symlink to /share/passwd, 4) Verify handle resolves to new location but STILL within share (not /etc/passwd). This catches the attack vector.

title: Server: remove obsolete security tests
description: Remove tests that checked path-string handles for security (path traversal, absolute paths, null bytes, empty handles). These are no longer relevant since Ulid handles are opaque. Tests: resolve_rejects_path_traversal, resolve_rejects_absolute_handle, resolve_rejects_null_byte, resolve_rejects_empty, etc.

title: Server: update handler.rs to use HandleDatabase
description: Modify handler.rs to use HandleDatabase for Ulid<->path resolution. Add parse_handle() function, update resolve(), stat_response(), lookup_response(), readdir_response() to use Ulid handles. Test handles use the new HandleDatabase.

title: Server: add HandleDatabase test helper
description: Add test helper function to create HandleDatabase in tests/server.rs. This helper will be used across multiple tests to create a pre-populated database with known handles for test files.

title: Server: implement HandleDatabase with scc HashIndex
description: Create crates/rift-server/src/handle.rs with HandleDatabase struct using scc::HashIndex. Stores Ulid<->PathBuf mappings, reads/writes 'user.rift.handle' xattr. Implement get_or_create_handle(), get_path(), get_ulid(), and populate_from_share(). Follow TDD: write failing tests first.

title: Workspace: add ulid, scc, xattr dependencies
description: Add ulid=1.1, scc, and xattr to workspace dependencies in Cargo.toml. These will be used by both rift-server and rift-client.

title: Fix e2e FUSE tests
description: Restore and fix the e2e_fuse_test.rs tests that are currently failing. Tests verify ls and cat work correctly with the new handle cache implementation.

title: FUSE: use cache for path resolution
description: Update FUSE getattr, lookup, readdir, read to use the ShareView handle cache instead of passing path strings directly. The cache resolves 'subdir/file.txt' to ULID handle bytes.

title: ShareView: add path_to_handle helper method
description: Add method to resolve a path string (e.g., 'subdir/file.txt') to handle bytes using the internal handle cache. Returns None if path not cached.

title: Client: cache handles from stat response
description: Update stat_batch to cache handles returned in StatResult.handle field. Handles stat'd files that weren't discovered via readdir/lookup.

title: Client: cache handles from lookup response
description: Update RiftShareView to cache handle from LookupResult.handle when lookup is called. Handles newly discovered files.

title: Client: cache handles from readdir response
description: Update RiftShareView to cache handles from ReaddirEntry.handle field when readdir is called. This populates the cache as the client traverses the filesystem.

title: Client: implement HandleCache for path<->Ulid mapping
description: Create HandleCache struct using scc HashIndex. Stores Ulid<->String mappings for paths relative to share root. Add to RiftClient.

title: Client: add ulid and scc dependencies
description: Add ulid and scc crate dependencies to rift-client for handle caching implementation

title: Server: return handle in stat response
description: Update server stat handler to populate the handle field in StatResult. This requires serializing the Ulid handle to bytes before sending over the wire.
close_reason: Done - handler already returns handle in stat

title: Protocol: add handle field to StatResult
description: Add  field (bytes) to StatResult proto message in operations.proto so stat responses include the handle alongside attrs. This enables client to cache handles for stat'd files.
close_reason: Done - protocol already has handle field

title: Phase 3: Rewrite and audit server handle tests
description: Rewrite tests in 'server.rs' to match new security model (symlinks outside share). Add ULID boundary tests parsing 0, 15, and 17 bytes to ensure graceful rejection. Make sure tests use '#[tokio::test]' where appropriate and 'HandleDatabase' usage is thoroughly tested.
close_reason: Superseded by new client migration plan

title: Phase 2: Enforce security boundaries at handle creation and remove legacy path resolution
description: Remove resolve_from_path entirely from handler.rs. Add 'share_root: &Path' to 'get_or_create_handle' in handle.rs. Verify that the canonicalized path of the entry starts with the canonicalized share root before generating a handle. If it escapes (e.g. symlink points outside), fail gracefully without a handle.
close_reason: Superseded by new client migration plan

title: Phase 1: Refactor internal handles to use Ulid type
description: Refactor resolve() and all request handlers (stat_response, lookup_response, etc.) in rift-server/src/handler.rs to accept and use ulid::Ulid instead of &[u8]. The conversion from Vec<u8> protobuf payloads to Ulid must happen immediately upon request decoding. Invalid lengths must immediately return ErrorUnsupported or ErrorNotFound without panicking.

title: (EPIC) Secure and Type-Safe ULID File Handles Refactor
description: Follow-up to the initial ULID handles implementation. Refactors internal handle representation to use the Ulid type for type-safety, removes the legacy path-based resolution fallback, enforces security boundaries (e.g. symlink escapes) at handle creation, and rewrites tests to match the new security model.
close_reason: Superseded by new client migration plan

title: Phase 4: Final Validation and E2E Test Pass
description: Remove the #[ignore] attribute from the end-to-end test created in Phase 0. Run the full client test suite ('cargo test -p rift-client'). The 'test_handles_are_persistent_and_rename_safe' test must now pass. Debug any integration issues until it does.

title: Phase 3: Refactoring Server Handlers (Unit Tests)
description: TDD Cycle: For each affected handler (resolve, lookup, readdir, handshake), write a failing unit test that proves it uses the HandleDatabase. Then, modify the handler to use the database and make the test pass. Remove old path-based tests.

title: Phase 2: DB Population from Filesystem Scan (Integration Test)
description: TDD Cycle: 1. Write a failing integration test that calls a 'populate_from_share' function. 2. Assert that the resulting HandleDatabase is correct (reads existing handles, creates new ones). 3. Implement the scanner logic using 'walkdir' to make the test pass. 4. Refactor.

title: Phase 1: Handle Generation and xattr Storage (Unit Tests)
description: TDD Cycle: 1. Write failing unit test for 'get_or_create_handle' to prove it writes a new handle xattr. 2. Implement the function to make it pass. 3. Write failing unit test to prove it reads an existing handle. 4. Implement to pass. 5. Refactor.

title: Phase 0: Setup and E2E Test Definition
description: 1. Add dependencies: ulid, xattr, walkdir, dashmap. 2. Create the empty HandleDatabase struct. 3. Write the final, #[ignored] end-to-end test in 'crates/rift-client/tests/e2e_handles.rs' that proves handles are persistent and rename-safe.

title: Implement persistent TLS certificates and TOFU policy for client
description:
Implement: (1) Load persistent client cert from ~/.config/rift/client.{cert,key} for stable fingerprint; (2) Use TofuPolicy from ~/.config/rift/known-servers.toml; (3) Load persistent certificate for stable fingerprint across restarts.

**Blocked by**: rift-odd (persistent client state in /var/lib/rift/)

**Dependencies**: Requires rift-odd to be done first (needs persistent state directory).
close_reason: Completed: connect_persistent uses TofuPolicy from known-servers.toml, reconnect preserves TOFU state, persistent cert via ClientPaths

title: (EPIC) Implement Cryptographic File Handles (ULID-based, xattr-backed)
description: Tracks the TDD implementation of persistent, opaque, and rename-safe file handles. The approach uses ULIDs stored in the 'user.rift.handle' extended attribute, cached in a concurrent in-memory database at server startup. This epic is broken down into child tasks representing each phase of the TDD process.

title: Implement server-side authorization (permissions, share validation, client fingerprint check)
description:
Implement server-side authorization: validate share name against configured shares, check client fingerprint against permission files, derive read_only flag from permission file, send typed ERROR_RESPONSE on protocol errors.

**Blocked by**: rift-5dt (server-side per-share authorization with per-cert access levels)

**Dependencies**: Requires per-share per-cert authorization (rift-5dt) to be in place first. This is the final piece of the auth chain: rift-apf → rift-9k6 → rift-5dt → rift-3lh.

title: Implement TLS certificate verification
description: Implement verification of CertificateVerify handshake transcript signature in TLS layer. Currently missing - needed for production security. Location: crates/rift-transport/src/tls.rs lines 78 and 145.
close_reason: verify_tls13_signature now calls rustls::crypto::verify_tls13_signature with ring provider; both server-side and client-side verifiers properly validate CertificateVerify signatures

title: Implement heartbeat/lease mechanism
description:
Implement heartbeat/lease mechanism. Client sends periodic heartbeats to maintain share lease. Server detects disconnected clients and releases locks.

**Blocked by**: rift-4kq (persistent TLS certs and TOFU policy)

**Dependencies**: Requires persistent certificates (rift-4kq) for stable identity.

title: Implement Merkle tree cache persistence on client
description:
Implement Merkle tree cache persistence on client. Store computed Merkle trees in local cache to enable delta sync without re-computing.

**Blocked by**: rift-odd (persistent client state in /var/lib/rift/)

**Dependencies**: Requires rift-odd (needs persistent cache directory).
close_reason: Completed: FileCache already persists via SQLite; added cache_persists_across_reopen unit test and client_cache_persists_across_sessions integration test to verify

title: Implement persistent client state in /var/lib/rift/
description:
Implement persistent client state storage in /var/lib/rift/ (or configurable path). Store sync state, transfer progress, cached metadata.

**No blockers** - Can be started immediately.

**Rationale**: Foundation for client-side state. Other client tasks depend on this.
close_reason: Completed: ClientPaths with XDG state dir, --state-dir CLI override, explicit cert/key paths in connect_with_cert

title: Implement lazy out-of-band change detection on server
description:
Implement lazy out-of-band change detection on server. On every client access, check mtime+size against cached metadata. If different, invalidate checksums.

**Blocked by**: rift-v49 (Merkle tree cache persistence on client)

**Dependencies**: Requires cached Merkle trees (rift-v49) to check validity.
close_reason: Completed: Server already detects out-of-band changes via mtime+size check in get_merkle(). Added stat_detects_out_of_band_file_modification and stat_detects_out_of_band_file_size_change integration tests.

title: Implement rift refresh CLI command for out-of-band change notification
description:
Implement 'rift refresh' CLI command. Tells server daemon to re-scan shares and notify clients of out-of-band changes.

**Blocked by**: rift-3n8 (lazy out-of-band change detection)

**Dependencies**: Needs server change detection (rift-3n8) to notify about.

title: Implement rift export CLI command (list/manage shares)
description: Implement  command to list available shares on a server and manage them. Should work similar to NFS exportfs.

title: Implement TOML configuration file for server
description: Implement TOML configuration file for rift-server. Replace CLI-only args with config file that defines: shares (path, name), TLS settings, client access permissions, identity mapping per share, etc.

title: Implement identity modes (fixed and mapped) for client UID mapping
description: Implement identity mapping modes on server side: (1) Fixed - all operations run as a single server-side uid/gid regardless of client identity; (2) Mapped - client UIDs mapped to server UIDs per explicit rules. Support root_squash option.

title: Implement server-side per-share authorization with per-cert access levels
description:
Implement server-side per-share authorization with per-cert access levels. Each share has an .allow file listing authorized client certificate fingerprints.

**Blocked by**: rift-9k6 (TLS client certificate authentication)

**Dependencies**: Requires TLS auth (rift-9k6) to be working first.

title: Implement TLS client certificate authentication
description:
Implement TLS client certificate authentication on server side. Server validates client certificates against trusted CA or pinned certs. Reject connections from unauthorized clients.

**Blocked by**: rift-apf (TLS certificate verification)

**Dependencies**: Requires TLS verification (rift-apf) to be in place first.

title: Implement delta sync via Merkle tree block comparison
description:
Implement delta sync - compare client's cached Merkle tree with server's to identify changed blocks. Only transfer changed blocks, not entire file.

**Blocked by**: rift-jdi (CDC chunking), rift-abh (Merkle tree)

**Uses**: MERKLE_COMPARE, MERKLE_DRILL, MERKLE_LEAVES protocol

title: Implement resume validation (fingerprint check: mtime + size)
description: Every resume request (read or write) must carry the original file's fingerprint: mtime + size as recorded when the transfer started. Server compares fingerprint against current file state before accepting resume. If mismatched (file modified out-of-band), reject with FILE_MODIFIED error.

title: Implement resumable transfers (read and write)
description:
Implement resumable transfers - if transfer interrupted (connection drop), client can resume from where it left off. Server retains partial data for resume retention window (default 1 hour).

**Blocked by**: rift-9l7 (CoW write semantics)

**Dependencies**: Requires CoW semantics (rift-9l7) to have consistent file state for resume.

title: Implement mtime+size fast-path validation
description:
Implement fast-path cache validation using mtime + size comparison.

Status (2025-04-20):
- STAT already uses cache via get_or_compute_merkle_root - verified working  
- MERKLE_DRILL level 0 fast-path: DEFERRED until MERKLE_DRILL protocol finalized

Deferred work:
- Add cache check in merkle_drill_response for level 0 only
- Level > 0 should NOT use cache (tree depth varies)

title: Implement Content-Defined Chunking (CDC) with FastCDC
description:
Implement FastCDC for content-defined chunking to replace fixed-offset blocks. This ensures delta sync efficiency - inserting/deleting bytes only affects 1-2 chunks near the edit point, not the entire file. Parameters: min=32KB, target=128KB, max=512KB.

**Status**: Core CDC chunking IMPLEMENTED in rift-common/crypto.rs (Chunker struct). Comprehensive unit + property tests pass. Server uses Chunker in Merkle tree computation.

**Remaining work (subtasks)**:
- rift-mr6: Per-share CDC configuration
- rift-ssu: Parallel chunking for large files

title: Implement block-level Merkle tree checksums with BLAKE3
description:
Implement block-level Merkle tree checksums with BLAKE3. Each CDC chunk gets a BLAKE3 hash; chunks combined into a 64-ary Merkle tree.

**Blocked by**: rift-jdi (CDC chunking core required for chunk boundaries)

title: Implement CoW write semantics (write to temp, fsync, rename)
description:
Implement copy-on-write write semantics: write to temp file on server, fsync, then atomically rename temp over original. Ensures zero write holes - readers see old or new version, never partial data.

**Blocked by**: rift-4ut (write locking with single-writer lock per file)

**Dependencies**: Requires write locking (rift-4ut) to coordinate writes.

title: Implement write locking with single-writer lock per file
description:
Implement single-writer lock per file on server. When client starts a write, acquire exclusive lock. Other clients can still read (CoW semantics). Lock has progress timeout (default 60s).

**Blocked by**: rift-d7v (delta sync via Merkle tree)

**Dependencies**: Needs delta sync (rift-d7v) to identify which blocks changed before locking.

title: Missing trailing newline in proto/transfer.proto
description: The proto file lost its trailing newline in the delta-sync branch. POSIX compliance issue — some tools may warn. Add trailing newline.

title: Fragile string matching for QueryReturnedNoRows in DB methods
description: Multiple methods in merkle.rs check for QueryReturnedNoRows by string-matching the error message: e.to_string().contains("Query returned no rows"). This was a bug fix (previously matched Debug format "QueryReturnedNoRows"), but string matching is fragile — breaks if tokio_rusqlite changes error messages. Investigate whether pattern-matching on the error variant directly is possible. If not, at least add a test that verifies the string still works.

title: O(n²) length patching in build_with_cache_and_offsets()
description: build_with_cache_and_offsets() fills in leaf lengths by iterating all cache values and for each leaf child, doing a linear scan of leaf_infos with .find(). With 10,000 chunks this is ~100M comparisons. Fix: build a HashMap<Blake3Hash, u64> from leaf_infos for O(1) lookup instead of O(n) linear scan per leaf.

title: Single-leaf build_with_cache() produces length: 0 in cache
description: When there's exactly 1 leaf, build_with_cache() creates root→[Leaf{hash: root, length: 0, chunk_index: 0}]. The length is always 0 until build_with_cache_and_offsets() patches it. Clients that call build_with_cache() directly get incorrect length data. Either: (a) document this as intentional and require callers to use build_with_cache_and_offsets(), or (b) accept length as a parameter in build_with_cache() too.

title: Add integration tests for hash-based Merkle drill
description: Add integration tests in rift-server/tests/server.rs. Tests: full sync file->tree->drill->verify children, query non-existent hash returns empty, rebuild on mtime change, drill to root returns children, drill to leaf returns metadata.

title: Add unit tests for ReconnectingClient methods
description: ReconnectingClient in crates/rift-client/src/reconnect.rs (line 29) has new(), reconnect(), close_connection_for_test() with zero unit tests. Only the helper is_connection_error() is tested (3 tests). Add tests: (1) new() wraps client correctly, (2) operations on wrapped client succeed, (3) after connection lost, auto-reconnect is triggered, (4) close_connection_for_test() causes subsequent ops to fail until reconnect, (5) reconnect() re-establishes connection. This requires a test server — use the common.rs helpers from tests/ or InMemoryListener.

title: Add unit tests for FUSE filesystem operations
description: RiftFilesystem in crates/rift-client/src/fuse.rs (line 56) has zero tests for actual FUSE operations. Only proto_to_fuse3_attr conversion is tested (4 tests). The FUSE ops (getattr, lookup, read, readdir, opendir, releasedir) are only tested via fuse_integration.rs. Add unit tests using a mock ShareView: (1) getattr calls view.getattr with correct path, (2) lookup calls view.lookup with parent+name, (3) read calls view.read with correct offset/size, (4) readdir returns formatted entries, (5) error propagation (NotFound → ENOENT). Create a MockShareView implementing the ShareView trait (already done in tests/fuse_integration.rs — can reuse pattern).

title: Add unit tests for RiftClient discover() and whoami()
description: RiftClient::discover() (line 805) and whoami() (line 832) in crates/rift-client/src/client.rs have zero unit tests. Add tests: (1) discover returns list of shares, (2) discover with empty share list, (3) whoami returns WhoamiResponse with fingerprint and common_name, (4) both methods fail appropriately on connection error. Use InMemoryConnection with server tasks.

title: Add unit tests for RiftClient stat_batch()
description: RiftClient::stat_batch() in crates/rift-client/src/client.rs (line 450) sends multiple handles in a single request. Integration tests exist in tests/integration_stat_batch.rs but no unit tests. Add unit tests: (1) verify stat_batch sends a single StatRequest with all handles (use RecordingConnection to inspect wire), (2) results are returned in same order as input handles, (3) empty input returns empty output, (4) single handle works, (5) connection error during batch returns Err. Use InMemoryConnection with server task responding appropriately.

title: Add tests for server.rs accept_loop() startup and shutdown
description: crates/rift-server/src/server.rs accept_loop() (line 47) is the main server loop that accepts connections and spawns handlers. ZERO tests. Add tests: (1) accept_loop starts and accepts a connection, (2) accept_loop handles client disconnect gracefully, (3) accept_loop processes multiple sequential connections, (4) accept_loop performs handshake before handling requests. Can use InMemoryListener from rift-transport for testing without real QUIC. The function takes a RiftListener + config, so it's testable with mocks.

title: Add tests for metadata/db.rs Database open() and error handling
description: crates/rift-server/src/metadata/db.rs Database has only 1 test (database_creates_schema). Missing: (1) open() creates database file if not exists, (2) open() with existing database reuses it, (3) open() with invalid path returns error, (4) open() creates parent directories if needed, (5) call() executes function on connection, (6) call() propagates errors from closure. The Database wraps tokio_rusqlite::Connection and is used by merkle.rs.

title: Add unit tests for handler.rs mutation responses (mkdir/unlink/rmdir)
description: crates/rift-server/src/handler.rs has mkdir_response() (line 355), unlink_response() (line 368), rmdir_response() (line 381). These currently return unsupported errors. Add unit tests: (1) mkdir_response returns appropriate error/response, (2) unlink_response returns appropriate error/response, (3) rmdir_response returns appropriate error/response. Even if currently unsupported, tests should verify the error type/code is correct. When write support is added, these tests will need to be updated to cover actual behavior.

title: Add unit tests for handler.rs merkle_drill_response()
description: crates/rift-server/src/handler.rs merkle_drill_response() (line 720) handles merkle tree drill requests for sync. ZERO unit tests. Add tests: (1) drill at level 0 returns root level hashes, (2) drill at level 1 with parent_indices returns child hashes, (3) drill on file with no cached merkle data, (4) drill with invalid handle, (5) verify hashes match what MerkleTree from rift-common would produce. Use InMemoryConnection + temp files with known content.

title: Add unit tests for handler.rs readdir_response()
description: crates/rift-server/src/handler.rs readdir_response() (line 258) handles readdir requests. Add unit tests: (1) directory with files returns all entries, (2) empty directory returns empty list, (3) directory with subdirectories, (4) offset parameter skips entries, (5) limit parameter caps results, (6) has_more flag set correctly when more entries exist, (7) invalid handle returns error. Use HandleDatabase + temp filesystem + InMemoryConnection.

title: Add unit tests for handler.rs lookup_response()
description: crates/rift-server/src/handler.rs lookup_response() (line 188) handles lookup requests. Add unit tests: (1) lookup existing file returns handle + attrs, (2) lookup nonexistent entry returns NotFound error, (3) lookup in nonexistent parent returns error, (4) lookup with empty name, (5) lookup with '..' (should be rejected or handled safely), (6) lookup of symlink returns symlink attrs. Use HandleDatabase + temp filesystem + InMemoryConnection.

title: Add unit tests for handler.rs stat_response()
description: crates/rift-server/src/handler.rs stat_response() (line 154) handles stat requests. Tested via integration but no unit tests. Add tests with InMemoryConnection: (1) valid handle returns FileAttrs with correct size/type/mode, (2) invalid handle returns error response, (3) batch stat with multiple handles returns results in order, (4) batch stat with mix of valid/invalid handles, (5) empty handles list. Use InMemoryConnection::pair() for stream, create temp files for real metadata.

title: Add unit tests for handler.rs build_attrs() and metadata_to_attrs()
description: crates/rift-server/src/handler.rs has metadata_to_attrs() (line 104) and build_attrs() (line 114). metadata_to_attrs is tested in integration tests but not for boundary conditions. build_attrs is not directly tested at all. Add tests: (1) metadata_to_attrs for regular file — verify size, mode, timestamps, (2) metadata_to_attrs for directory, (3) metadata_to_attrs for symlink, (4) build_attrs adds root_hash correctly, (5) build_attrs handles zero-length file, (6) timestamp conversion correctness (seconds + nanoseconds). Create temp files/dirs to get real std::fs::Metadata values.

title: Add handshake edge case tests for malformed data
description: crates/rift-transport/src/handshake.rs has client_handshake(), recv_hello(), send_welcome() functions. Integration tests cover happy path. Missing edge cases: (1) recv_hello with truncated protobuf payload, (2) recv_hello with empty payload, (3) recv_hello with oversized payload, (4) client_handshake when server sends malformed welcome, (5) client_handshake when server closes stream before responding. Use InMemoryConnection from connection.rs for these tests.

title: Add direct tests for tls.rs certificate/endpoint functions
description: crates/rift-transport/src/tls.rs has zero direct tests. Functions to test: (1) server_endpoint() — create with valid cert/key DER, verify it returns Ok, (2) client_endpoint() — create with valid cert/key, verify Ok, (3) client_endpoint_no_cert() — verify it works without cert, (4) AcceptAnyClientCertVerifier — verify it accepts any certificate, (5) PolicyServerCertVerifier — verify it delegates to the policy. Use rcgen to generate test certs (same pattern as tests/quic_transport.rs gen_test_cert helper). These are currently only tested transitively through QUIC integration tests.

title: Add tests for TransportError and CertError enums
description: crates/rift-transport/src/error.rs defines TransportError (6 variants: ConnectionFailed, ConnectionClosed, StreamError, HandshakeError, CertificateError, Timeout) and CertError (2 variants: NotTrusted, FingerprintChanged). ZERO tests exist. Add tests: (1) Display output for each variant, (2) From<CertError> for TransportError conversion, (3) From<std::io::Error> if implemented, (4) Debug output is non-empty. These error types are used throughout the crate.

title: Add roundtrip tests for notification messages (0x60-0x66)
description: ALL notification messages in crates/rift-protocol/src/messages.rs have ZERO tests: FILE_CHANGED (0x60), FILE_CREATED (0x61), FILE_DELETED (0x62), FILE_RENAMED (0x63), DIR_CREATED (0x64), DIR_DELETED (0x65), DIR_RENAMED (0x66). Add roundtrip encode/decode tests for each. Each should verify all fields (handle, path, old_path for renames, timestamps). This is critical because the entire notification subsystem is currently assumed broken.

title: Add roundtrip tests for merkle protocol messages
description: MERKLE_DRILL (0x50), MERKLE_LEVEL_RESPONSE (0x51), MERKLE_LEAVES_RESPONSE (0x52) in crates/rift-protocol/src/messages.rs have no roundtrip tests. Add tests: (1) MerkleDrill request with handle, level, parent_indices, (2) MerkleLevelResponse with hashes and sizes, (3) MerkleLeavesResponse with leaf hashes. These messages are used in the merkle tree sync protocol and are exercised at higher levels but need direct protocol-level verification.

title: Add roundtrip tests for read/write transfer messages
description: Messages READ_REQUEST (0x30), READ_RESPONSE (0x31), BLOCK_HEADER (0x32), TRANSFER_COMPLETE (0x33), WRITE_REQUEST (0x34), WRITE_COMMIT (0x35), WRITE_RESPONSE (0x36) in crates/rift-protocol/src/messages.rs have no direct roundtrip tests. For write: commit message, response with success/error. For block_header and transfer_complete: verify all fields survive roundtrip.

title: Add roundtrip tests for SETATTR_REQUEST/RESPONSE messages
description: SETATTR_REQUEST (0x20) and SETATTR_RESPONSE (0x21) in crates/rift-protocol/src/messages.rs have no tests. Add tests: (1) setattr request with mode change, (2) setattr request with size change (truncate), (3) setattr request with timestamp change, (4) setattr response success with updated attrs, (5) setattr response error. Follow the encode-with-prost/decode pattern used by other message tests in the same file.

title: Stream file reads on the server instead of loading entire file into memory
description:
The server reads the entire file into memory before processing it in three handlers (crates/rift-server/src/handler.rs):
- get_or_compute_merkle_root (line ~430): tokio::fs::read(path) to compute Merkle hash
- read_response (line ~543): tokio::fs::read(path) to send chunks
- merkle_drill_response (line ~713): tokio::fs::read(path) to compute leaf hashes

For large files (multi-GB) this will OOM the server. read_response is the most critical: it should open the file and stream chunks directly to the wire without buffering the whole file.

What needs to be done:
1. read_response: replace tokio::fs::read with tokio::fs::File::open, then read+send each chunk independently using tokio::io::AsyncReadExt. FastCDC chunking (Chunker::chunk) currently operates on a complete &[u8] — you will need to evaluate whether to: (a) mmap the file so Chunker still works on a slice, or (b) replace Chunker with a streaming CDC variant, or (c) read the file once into memory just for chunking boundary computation, then stream actual chunk data from file by seeking.
2. get_or_compute_merkle_root (called from stat): same issue, lower urgency since stat is less frequent. Option (c) from above (read for CDC, then discard buffer after hashing) is a pragmatic interim if full streaming is deferred.
3. merkle_drill_response: same as #2.

Note: the FastCDC Chunker in rift-common::crypto::Chunker takes &[u8] — assess whether it can be changed to an iterator/streaming API first, as that's the enabling change for true streaming in all three handlers.

The Chunker is in crates/rift-common/src/crypto.rs.

title: Wire up _cached_root_hash in ShareView::read to skip stat round-trip
description:
ShareView::read (crates/rift-client/src/view.rs:186) accepts cached_root_hash: Option<&[u8]> but immediately binds it as _cached_root_hash and discards it. The intent is to let the FUSE layer pass an already-known root hash (from the inode cache) so the mandatory stat_batch round-trip at line 195 can be skipped.

What needs to be done:
1. In view.rs::read, if cached_root_hash is Some(hash), skip the stat_batch call and use (file_size=u64::MAX as sentinel or require caller to supply it too) — or alternatively change the signature to accept cached attrs (size + root_hash together, since size is also needed to clamp the read range at line 226/249).
2. The cleanest API change: replace cached_root_hash: Option<&[u8]> with cached_attrs: Option<&FileAttrs>, so the caller can supply both size and root_hash. Skip stat_batch when Some.
3. Update the ShareView trait definition (crates/rift-client/src/view.rs:38) and all trait impls/callers, including the FUSE layer (crates/rift-client/src/fuse/ or platform-specific mount code).
4. Update MockRemote in view.rs tests to not set a stat_batch result when the cached path is taken.

The optimization matters for sequential reads of the same file: currently every read() call pays a full stat RPC round-trip even when the FUSE inode cache already has fresh attrs.

title: stat_response uses blocking std::fs calls inside join_all on async runtime
description:
In crates/rift-server/src/handler.rs, stat_response was refactored to use futures::future::join_all for concurrency. However, resolve() calls std::fs::canonicalize and the body calls std::fs::metadata — both synchronous blocking operations. Running blocking calls inside join_all on tokio tasks blocks the async runtime thread pool, preventing other tasks from making progress.

Fix: Replace std::fs::metadata with tokio::fs::metadata. For resolve(), which calls std::fs::canonicalize, wrap the call in tokio::task::spawn_blocking so it runs on the blocking thread pool. Alternatively, revert to a sequential for-loop if concurrency is not needed (the previous for-loop was sequential and worked fine).
acceptance_criteria: No std::fs calls remain in the async stat_response hot path (except behind spawn_blocking); Tests continue to pass
close_reason: Replaced all blocking std::fs calls with tokio::fs equivalents; resolve() now async; no std::fs calls remain in handler hot path

title: HandleDatabase grows unboundedly; no eviction for deleted/renamed paths
description:
In crates/rift-server/src/handle.rs, HandleDatabase is an in-memory BidirectionalMap that maps ULIDs ↔ relative paths. Entries are only added (via get_or_create_handle and populate_from_share); they are never removed. When a file is deleted or renamed by the share owner, the stale ULID still resolves to an old relative path — the subsequent canonicalize call will fail (error, but still wasteful), and the map entry persists indefinitely. Over time with many file operations this is a memory leak.

The old path-based approach had the same problem (dead paths accumulate), but with ULIDs there's no way for the client to "refresh" a handle — it's opaque.

Fix: Implement cleanup strategies, e.g.:
- On resolve failure (canonicalize returns ENOENT), proactively remove the stale ULID from the map.
- Add a periodic or lru-based eviction for entries not recently accessed.
- Document the current limitation and planned fix.

Start with the minimal fix: remove the entry when resolve() fails due to a missing path. This prevents stale entries from accumulating on repeated access attempts.
acceptance_criteria: When resolve fails because the path no longer exists, the stale ULID is removed from the BidirectionalMap; A test verifies that a deleted file's handle is cleaned up on failed resolve; get_or_create_handle correctly re-creates a handle if the old one was evicted
close_reason: Added HandleDatabase::remove(), resolve() evicts stale handles on ENOENT and symlink escape; tests verify eviction and re-creation

title: Refactor HandleDatabase to use canonicalized full paths instead of relative paths
description:
## Current State

HandleDatabase currently uses relative paths (stripping share root prefix) for ULID lookups. This has edge cases with:
- Empty paths (share root becomes  instead of .)
- Repeating path patterns (e.g., /tmp/xxx/tmp/xxx/)
- Path ambiguity issues

## Proposed Refactor

Switch to using canonicalized full paths as keys in HandleDatabase:

### Pros:
1. Simpler code - no strip_prefix logic, no edge cases
2. Direct lookup - canonical path is the key
3. xattr-stored ULIDs still provide file identity persistence
4. Security maintained by forbidding absolute symlinks + canonicalize + starts_with check

### Implementation Steps:

1. **Update HandleDatabase methods**:
   -  - use canonical(path) as key instead of relative path
   -  - returns canonical path directly
   -  - populate with canonical paths

2. **Add symlink restriction**:
   - At : skip or error on absolute symlinks
   - At : check if path is absolute symlink, reject if so

3. **Update resolve()**:
   - Look up canonical path from ULID
   - Canonicalize again (catches changes)
   - Verify starts_with(share_canonical) - security check

4. **Update all tests**:
   - Handler unit tests
   - Integration tests
   - New symlink security tests

### Acceptance Criteria:
- All existing tests pass
- New test: absolute symlink is rejected
- Simpler path handling code (no strip_prefix edge cases)
- Performance equivalent or better

### Notes:
- This is NOT about changing the wire protocol (handles are still 16-byte ULIDs)
- This is internal storage representation change
- xattr storage still provides persistence across restarts
- Security invariant: starts_with check must remain in resolve()
close_reason: HandleDatabase now stores canonical paths; share_root param removed; resolve() simplified; strip_prefix edge cases eliminated

title: Replace std::sync::Mutex with tokio::sync::Mutex in server and transport
description:
The codebase uses both std::sync::Mutex and tokio::sync::Mutex. The std::sync::Mutex is used in async contexts (server database and transport policy), which can block the tokio runtime thread.

Locations:
- rift-server/src/metadata/db.rs: Database struct uses std::sync::Mutex<Connection>
- rift-transport/src/policy.rs: TofuPolicy uses std::sync::Mutex<TofuStore>

This is problematic because blocking on std::sync::Mutex in async code can starve other tasks on the same worker thread.

Fix: Replace with tokio::sync::Mutex, using .lock().await instead of .lock().unwrap()

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Replace std::sync::Mutex in db.rs with tokio::sync::Mutex
- [ ] Replace std::sync::Mutex in policy.rs with tokio::sync::Mutex
- [ ] Update Database::connection() to return async guard
- [ ] Run tests to verify
notes:
The issue with updating Database::connection():

1. Current: Returns std::sync::MutexGuard directly
2. With tokio::sync::Mutex: Returns tokio::sync::MutexGuard
3. tokio::sync::MutexGuard is NOT Sync - this affects Database's Send+Sync bounds
4. The server passes Database as Option<&Database> to sync handler functions

Workaround: Either wrap in Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<Connection>> or keep synchronous usage pattern but switch to tokio::sync::Mutex anyway (still better than std::sync in async context).

title: HandleDatabase silently ignores xattr write failures
description:
In crates/rift-server/src/handle.rs line 100, xattr::set failures are discarded with . This means:
- On filesystems that support xattrs but hit a permissions error, the failure is invisible — handles won't persist across restarts, and the operator has no way to diagnose why.
- On filesystems that don't support xattrs (e.g. tmpfs in tests), the best-effort silence is correct, but there's no distinction between "expected failure" and "unexpected failure."

Fix:
- Log a warning when xattr::set fails with an error other than ENOTSUP/EOPNOTSUPP (i.e. distinguish "expected" vs "unexpected" failures).
- For xattr::get (line 88), similarly log a warning on unexpected I/O errors (currently Ok(None) and Err both fall into the same "generate new ULID" branch).
acceptance_criteria: xattr::set failures that are NOT due to unsupported filesystem produce a tracing::warn log message; xattr::get errors that are NOT ENOTSUP also produce a warning; Handles still work correctly on filesystems without xattr support

title: Remove ad-hoc timeout on STAT_RESPONSE send; apply consistently or not at all
description:
In crates/rift-server/src/server.rs handle_stream, the msg::STAT_REQUEST branch has a 5-second tokio::time::timeout wrapping send_frame + finish_send, while all other message branches (LOOKUP, READDIR, READ, MERKLE_DRILL) send without any timeout. This inconsistency has no documented justification.

If the concern is disconnected clients, the timeout should be applied consistently to all send paths (or better, at the connection level). If there is no evidence that send_frame can hang, the timeout adds complexity and a failure mode (dropping valid responses that take >5s on slow connections).

Fix: Remove the STAT-specific timeout. If client-disconnect resilience is needed, implement it as a connection-level concern (e.g. a write deadline on the QUIC stream) rather than per-message timeouts scattered through the handler. The other branches already demonstrate the simpler pattern of just propagating the error via .
acceptance_criteria: All message branches use the same send pattern (no special-casing STAT); No per-message timeouts in handle_stream
close_reason: Removed ad-hoc STAT timeout; all message branches now use consistent send_frame().await? + finish_send().await? pattern

title: Migrate from custom ULID to UUID v7 for file handles
description: Migrate the current custom ULID implementation in crates/rift-server/src/handle.rs to the standardized UUID v7 format (RFC 9562). UUID v7 provides the same time-ordered, 128-bit properties as ULID but with IETF standardization, better ecosystem support (uuid crate with 200M+ downloads), and improved tooling/debugging capabilities. The wire format remains identical (16 bytes binary), so this change is completely transparent to the client protocol.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Replace custom Ulid struct with uuid::Uuid using v7 feature
   2. Update HandleDatabase to use Uuid type instead of [u8; 16]
   3. Maintain 16-byte binary xattr storage (fully compatible)
   4. Update all handler code to use Uuid type
   5. Switch string representations to standard hyphenated UUID format
   6. All existing tests pass without modification
   7. Add uuid crate dependency with v7 feature to workspace

title: Implement reconnection cache sync
description:
Implement reconnection cache sync. On reconnect, compare client's cached Merkle tree with server's to identify what changed during disconnect.

**Blocked by**: rift-40i (resumable transfers), rift-v49 (Merkle tree cache persistence)

**Dependencies**: Needs both resumable transfers AND cached Merkle trees.

title: Implement cache leases (RIFT_LEASES)
description: Strong cache coherency guarantee beyond optimistic caching. Server grants read lease alongside file access - client knows cached version is current within lease window. Lease revocation before write commit ensures no staleness. Default 60s lease duration. See docs/01-requirements/features/leases.md.

title: Implement write-side file deduplication (RIFT_FILE_DEDUP)
description: When client writes a file whose content already exists elsewhere on server (identical copy under different name), skip full transfer. Client includes new file's Merkle root in WRITE_REQUEST. Server checks if any file already has that root, if so copies locally instead of receiving data. See docs/01-requirements/features/write-dedup.md.

title: Implement configurable Merkle tree fanout
description: Make Merkle tree fanout configurable per-share (advertised in RiftWelcome). Current default is 64. Higher fanout (256, 512) reduces tree depth for large files (important for WAN), lower fanout reduces per-level message size. No capability flag needed - server-advertised parameter. See docs/01-requirements/features/merkle-fanout-config.md.

title: Implement bandwidth throttling and scheduling
description: Implement rate limiting for transfers over WAN. Client-side: --bandwidth-limit, --upload-limit, --download-limit. Server-side: per-share or per-client limits in config. Time-based scheduling: throttle during business hours, unlimited at night. No protocol changes needed - implemented at QUIC transport layer. See docs/01-requirements/features/bandwidth-throttling.md.

title: Implement offline mode with conflict detection
description: Allow Rift mount to stay available when network disconnects. Files in cached/pinned state remain readable. Writes are journaled locally and synced on reconnect. Conflicts (both client and server modified) are resolved by saving client version as .rift-conflict file. Requires selective sync and Merkle tree. See docs/01-requirements/features/offline-mode.md.

title: Implement native Linux kernel module (replace FUSE)
description: Replace FUSE client with native kernel filesystem module for better performance. Architecture: hybrid kernel/userspace (rift.ko handles VFS, userspace daemon handles QUIC/TLS). Biggest gains: metadata operations (2-10x), small random I/O (2-5x), mmap (5-20x). See docs/01-requirements/features/kernel-module.md for detailed architecture and performance analysis.

title: Implement readdir glob filter (RIFT_READDIR_FILTER)
description: Implement server-side glob filtering for readdir. Client sends a glob pattern, server filters entries before returning. Reduces network transfer for large directories with filtering. See docs/01-requirements/features/readdir-filter.md.

title: Implement case-insensitive filenames (RIFT_CASE_INSENSITIVE)
description: Implement case-insensitive filename support for filesystems that need it (e.g., macOS-compatible shares). Requires handling case collisions (two files with same name, different case). See docs/01-requirements/features/case-insensitive.md.

title: Implement supplementary group mapping (RIFT_SUPGROUPS)
description: Implement supplementary group mapping for client UIDs. Currently server-side identity inherits whatever supplementary groups that user already has on the server. This feature allows explicit mapping of client supplementary groups to server groups. See docs/01-requirements/features/supplementary-groups.md.

title: Implement ACL support (RIFT_ACLS)
description: Implement Access Control Lists for fine-grained permissions beyond POSIX mode bits. Important for enterprise adoption when multiple clients with different users access the same share. Requires deciding between POSIX ACLs vs NFSv4 ACLs. See docs/01-requirements/features/acls.md for details.

title: Create rift CLI crate
description:
Create a new 'rift' crate (rift-cli) that serves as the single entry point for all rift CLI commands. Move functionality from rift-client and rift-server binaries into this crate.

Commands to support:
- rift mount - mount a remote share (currently in rift-client)
- rift server - server management commands:
  - rift server start - start the server
  - rift server regenerate-certificate - regenerate the server certificate

This consolidates CLI handling in one place and provides a unified command structure.

title: Migrate TofuPolicy to tokio::sync::Mutex
description:
The TofuPolicy in rift-transport/src/policy.rs uses std::sync::Mutex which can block in async context.

Replace with tokio::sync::Mutex to fix the blocking issue.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Replace std::sync::Mutex with tokio::sync::Mutex in policy.rs
- [ ] Update lock() calls to use .lock().await
- [ ] Tests pass

title: Migrate rift-client FileCache to tokio-rusqlite
description:
Refactor FileCache in rift-client/src/cache/db.rs to use tokio-rusqlite instead of rusqlite with std::sync::Mutex.

This is related to rift-41n (decide what to do with FileCache) - after that decision is made, implement with tokio-rusqlite.

Changes needed (if keeping FileCache):
1. FileCache uses tokio_rusqlite::Connection directly
2. All methods become async
3. Remove std::sync::Mutex entirely

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] If FileCache is kept: migrate to tokio-rusqlite
- [ ] Tests pass
close_reason: tokio-rusqlite migration done. Integration remains in rift-50j

title: Integrate FileCache into client
description:
FileCache in rift-client/src/cache/db.rs is unused. Integrate it into the client for caching manifests and chunks.

The cache should store:
- File manifests: (handle -> root_hash, chunk list)
- Chunk data: (chunk_hash -> data) content-addressable storage

This enables the client to cache data from the server and avoid re-fetching.

After integration, migrate to tokio-rusqlite (see rift-e79).

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Integrate FileCache into client (RiftClient or ShareView)
- [ ] Write tests for cache behavior
- [ ] Pass to rift-e79 for tokio-rusqlite migration

title: Investigate and decide what to do with unused FileCache in rift-client
description:
The FileCache struct in rift-client/src/cache/db.rs appears to be unused in the codebase - no imports or references found. It wraps a std::sync::Mutex<Connection>.

Current state:
-rift-client/src/lib.rs exports the cache module
- rift-client/src/cache/db.rs defines FileCache with std::sync::Mutex
- No code actually uses this cache

Options to decide:
1. Remove as dead code - delete the module entirely
2. Integrate into client - actually use it for caching manifests/chunks
3. Keep but fix the Mutex type - leave it unused but fix the std::sync::Mutex issue

We should decide which path to take before fixing the Mutex issue.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Decide on path forward (remove/integrate/keep)
- [ ] If integrating: specify where/how it should be used
- [ ] If removing: create cleanup task
close_reason: Decision made: integrate FileCache into client

Implement pluggable storage backends
description: Abstract server storage layer behind a trait. Enables S3/object storage, database-backed metadata, in-memory backends for testing, future clustered/erasure-coded storage. Server defines StorageBackend trait with operations matching protocol needs (stat, lookup, readdir, create, read, write, etc.). See docs/01-requirements/features/pluggable-backends.md.

title: Implement multi-server striping for increased throughput
description: Stripe data across multiple servers for increased throughput and capacity, similar to pNFS (parallel NFS). Metadata server directs clients to data servers. Dramatically increases complexity - far future post-v1 feature. See docs/01-requirements/features/multi-server-striping.md.

title: Implement partial file updates (sub-file writes)
description: Allow updating specific byte ranges without rewriting entire file. Instead of contiguous bytes, server stores ordered list of chunk references. Changing chunks 50-52 of 1000-chunk file: client sends 3 new chunks, server stores in content-addressed store, updates reference list. Eliminates full file read/write for small edits. See docs/01-requirements/features/partial-writes.md.

title: Implement file versioning (time travel)
description: Built-in version history for files. On each write commit, server retains previous Merkle root, timestamp, sequence number. Users can list versions, restore previous versions, browse old directory states. Retention policy configurable per-share (days, versions count). Uses existing content-addressed chunk storage. See docs/01-requirements/features/file-versioning.md.

title: Implement share-level access tokens
description: Generate time-limited tokens for ad-hoc share access. Token includes permissions (ro/rw), expiry, optional subdirectory scope. Recipients use token in URL (rift://server/share?token=...) without needing certificates. Signed structure validated by server. See docs/01-requirements/features/access-tokens.md.

title: Implement cross-share deduplication
description: Content-addressed chunk storage - identical chunks across shares/files stored once. Uses existing BLAKE3 hashes. Server maintains chunk store keyed by hash with reference counting. Client cache also content-addressed - if chunk already cached from any share, don't re-fetch. Opt-in and admin-controlled. See docs/01-requirements/features/cross-share-dedup.md.
